title: Dense Bi-Encoder Retrieval and Domain Transfer · Program Brief: Rag Domain Language Transfer
summary: A semantic search prototype embeds queries and documents identically without checking model instructions, returns top five from a large corpus, and has never tested domain or language transfer. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the domain language transfer increment in dense retrieval candidate service. Learne…
objectives: Evaluate dense retrieval on target domain, jargon, languages, intents, dates, and changed cases before accepting model-card results.
Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Rag Domain Language Transfer, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to dense retrieval candidate service.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-dense-retrieval-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-dense-retrieval-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_dense_retrieval_4(query_model: str = "retriever-v4", document_model: str = "retriever-v4", candidate_count: int = 50, relevant_rank: int = 4, domain_evaluated: bool = True, multilingual_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if query_model != document_model:
        return False, 0, "model-space-mismatch"
    if candidate_count <= 0 or relevant_rank < 1 or relevant_rank > candidate_count:
        return False, relevant_rank, "candidate-rank"
    if not domain_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "domain-transfer"
    if not multilingual_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "language-transfer"
    recall_proxy = candidate_count - relevant_rank + 1
    assert recall_proxy > 0
    return True, recall_proxy, "dense-candidates"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate dense retrieval on target domain, jargon, languages, intents, dates, and changed cases before accepting model-card results.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Domain Language Transfer model · Theory Rag Domain Language Transfer, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Domain Language Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Domain Language Transfer model · Theory Rag Domain Language Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6669 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dense retrieval on one embedding space, candidate recall, domain, and language evidence; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate dense retrieval on target domain, jargon, languages, intents, dates, and changed cases before accepting model-card results.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Domain Language Transfer model · Theory Rag Domain Language Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A public benchmark score transfers unchanged to a private corpus.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: rag-domain-language-transfer-ip67nw.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-domain-language-transfer-ip67nw(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_domain_language_transfer_ip67nw\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?query_model != document_model)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?relevant_rank > candidate_count)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not domain_evaluated)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not multilingual_evaluated)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using evaluate and dense plus specific case evidence.
